title: Well-posedness of the equations of a viscoelastic fluid with a free   boundary
summary:
In this article, we prove the local well-posedness, for arbitrary initial
data with certain regularity assumptions, of the equations of a Viscoelastic
Fluid of Johnson-Segalman type with a free surface. More general constitutive
laws can be easily managed in the same way. The geometry is defined by a solid
fixed bottom and an upper free boundary submitted to surface tension. The proof
relies on a Lagrangian formulation. First we solve two intermediate problems
through a fixed point using mainly G. Allain Appl. Math. Optim. 16 (1987) 37-50
for the Navier-Stokes part. Then we solve the whole Lagrangian problem on
$[0,T_0]$ for $T_0$ small enough through a contraction mapping. Since the
Lagrangian solution is smooth, we can come back to an Eulerian one.

title: Issues of Chaos and Recurrence in Infinite Dimensions
comment:
Relevant manuscripts arXiv:0707.4458 and arXiv:0707.4456 [The
  Poincar\'e Recurrence Problem of Inviscid Incompressible Fluids, Asian J.
  Math, vol.13, no.1, 7-14, (2009). A Recurrence Theorem on the Solutions to
  the 2D Euler Equation Asian J. Math, vol.13, no.1, 1-6, (2009)]
summary:
Various issues with regard to chaos and recurrence in infinite dimensions are
discussed. The doctrine we are trying to derive is that Sobolev spaces over
bounded spatial domains do host chaos and recurrence, while Sobolev spaces over
unbounded spatial domains are lack of chaos and recurrence. Local Sobolev
spaces over unbounded spatial domains can host chaos and are natural phase
spaces e.g. for fluid problems, but are very challenging to study.

title: On Pointwise Gradient Estimates for the Complex Monge-Ampere Equation
summary:
In this note, a gradient estimate for the complex Monge-Ampere equation is
established. It differs from previous estimates of Yau, Hanani, Blocki, P.
Guan, B. Guan - Q. Li in that it is pointwise, and depends only on the infimum
of the solution instead of its $C^0$ norm.

title: Branching analysis of a countable family of global similarity solutions   of a fourth-order thin film equation
summary:
We show that various asymptotic properties of global solutions of a
fourth-order quasilinear thin film equation can be described by branching from
corresponding solutions of the linear bi-harmonic equation. This includes a
countable family of self-similar solutions and other aspects.
